flags.message: The committed metrics/statpack.md lacks the 'Modern discretionary-cert petitions by disposition' section that .github/prompts/predict.md tells cert predictors to anchor on (statpack.json has only overall/by-court/by-topic/by-originating-circuit/by-Term sections). Fell back to the overall SCOTUS resolved rates (granted 1.4%) plus general knowledge of the modern cert grant rate. Either refresh the statpack with the cert-stage cut or adjust the prompt.

The configured CourtListener MCP server never became available in this cell (tool search surfaced no CourtListener tools after the server was reported as connecting). Zero MCP calls were made; the prediction proceeded on the provisioned inputs, corpus CLI, statpack, and one web search, per the degraded-upstream rule.

Pre-existing on main (commit 22e36fe, 'live: refresh corpus + outcomes'): 'fedcourts validate data' fails with 51 referential problems — orphan outcome.json files (e.g. scotus/72598770, scotus/73250314, 18 more listed) whose events have no event.yaml in the ledger. Not caused by, and untouchable from, this predictor cell (out of my write lane), but it may fail this run PR's required validate check. My own subtree validates clean (fedcourts validate on this case: OK, 4 artifacts).
